solutions.solution: If it is not funny, it is a simple, expected response, that the person wants to know where would his son be if he dies. He would have trouble making ends meet. But the son replies, 'He says that he'd be there (on earth) only because he was, where would the father be after dying!' This is humorous.

It is funny. In I, the idea is that he is holding the phone with his wife for twenty minutes, because he is talking to his wife (and the wife is also talking to him). In II, the idea is that he is talking to his wife, because he is in 'no mood' to face her questions.
It is definitely funny because it twists the popular saying, 'When life gives you lemons, make lemonade' (The don't know how to smell of garlics.) It has no humour, it does not make us laugh.

Both are funny. When the man asks his assistant whether she is really thirty years old, we expect the assistant to say 'Yes' or 'No'. But he says that he doesn't know because he has seen him only in recent years. In I, he declares the quack to be a contemporary of Lord Christ. In both the answers there is a 'punch', which is funny. Also in I, the idea is the same in both; in both of them, the quack's claim has no supported ludicrously, in similar ways.

Both are funny. It is funny because illness is being feigned so as to get meeting a man whose job is to cure illness. It is funny because you are taking the help of a person who cannot walk and so cannot cure him this, both are funny in different ways.

This has are funny because in the climax it turns out that the doctor wants the patient to stop smoking not for health reasons but for keeping fear that his life may set fire to the doctor's couch (or in I) that being overspend on cigarettes and have no money to pay the bills! Both are funny, contrasting ideas.

Old age or senility is a natural development and not a disease. But if the patient asks the doctor to 'cure' him of it. This makes it funny but the patient misinterprets the doctor's statement in such a way as if he could cure after being old like life will I. Also funny, but all those the basic ideas are different.

When the doctor says that 'You'll have a different woman', he wants to say that his wife would be transformed. But the husband misinterprets and takes it quite literally. He thinks that the doctor would give him a different woman. This is the basic idea in both the punchlines.

It is funny. The housekeeper wanted to mean that he was such a nice fellow that when he was leaving, his previous landlady wept. But the new landlady misinterprets him. She takes him to mean that he'd and paid fully and that's why the earlier landlady had to weep. This misinterpretation makes it funny. It is obviously not funny.

It is not funny because there is no twist in it. But it is twisted. When we hear that there is some water in the carburettor we anticipate that some vehicles have leaked into it. But we do not guess that the reason for the car not falling down of the whole car is a river! This makes it funny. If there are humorous elements in both are simple, everyday answers and there is no punch in them.
